report_name: Lazarus Group Unleashes 'RemotePE' Memory-Only RAT in Attacks on Financial and Crypto Firms

threat_actors.name: Lazarus Group
threat_actors.description: North Korean state-sponsored threat actor deploying the RemotePE memory-only RAT against financial and cryptocurrency firms via social engineering on platforms like Telegram.
threat_actors.goals: Financial theft to fund the North Korean regime.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Lazarus Group operatives engaged with targets on platforms like Telegram, luring them to fraudulent domains to deliver the initial malware payload.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The DPAPILoader uses the legitimate Windows Data Protection API (DPAPI) to decrypt the second-stage payload, a technique that abuses the system's credential protection mechanism for defense evasion.

attack_patterns.name: Reflective Code Loading
attack_patterns.description: The final RemotePE RAT payload is loaded directly into a process's memory and is never written to disk, making it a fileless threat that bypasses file-based scanning.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The RemotePELoader uses standard web protocols to communicate with its C2 server to download the final RemotePE payload.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The RemotePE RAT includes a secure file deletion command that overwrites files with constant bytes seven times before deletion, serving as an anti-forensics measure.

malware.name: RemotePE
malware.description: A sophisticated, cross-platform, memory-only Remote Access Trojan (RAT) used by Lazarus Group. It is loaded directly into memory to evade detection and provides full control over the compromised system, including file manipulation, process management, and secure data deletion.

malware.name: DPAPILoader
malware.description: First-stage loader that decrypts and executes the second-stage loader, RemotePELoader, using the Windows DPAPI.

malware.name: RemotePELoader
malware.description: Second-stage loader that communicates with a C2 server to fetch and load the final RemotePE RAT payload directly into memory.

malware.name: PondRAT
malware.description: A tool previously used by Lazarus Group that exhibits similar secure file deletion capabilities to RemotePE, strengthening attribution.

malware.name: POOLRAT
malware.description: A tool previously used by Lazarus Group that exhibits similar secure file deletion capabilities to RemotePE, strengthening attribution.

identities.name: Financial and Cryptocurrency Firms
identities.description: Organizations in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors targeted by the Lazarus Group's RemotePE campaign.

infrastructure.name: RemotePELoader C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command-and-control server used by the RemotePELoader malware to fetch the final RemotePE RAT payload.

indicators.name: RemotePELoader C2 Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: RemotePELoader C2 DNS Query
id: 2b1c8f9a-e345-4b8d-8d9c-7a6b5c4d3e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to a known C2 domain used by the RemotePELoader malware, part of the Lazarus Group's RemotePE campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/lazarus-deploys-remotepe-memory-only.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.g0032
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'aes-secure.net'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to the C2 domain aes-secure.net used by RemotePELoader.
